text.title: pnpm Workspaces with Dependency Catalogs Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the catalog protocol, workspace linkage, and skip path for pnpm monorepos.

rule.statement: External deps that appear in two or more workspace packages MUST be declared once under `catalog:` in `pnpm-workspace.yaml` and referenced as `"<pkg>": "catalog:"` in every package.json.
rule.rationale: Catalogs make version drift impossible by construction — exactly one place a version is defined.

rule.statement: Internal workspace packages MUST be referenced as `"<pkg>": "workspace:*"` (or `workspace:^`) in dependent packages.
rule.rationale: workspace: protocol ensures pnpm symlinks the local package instead of fetching from registry.

rule.statement: Hard-coded versions (e.g., `"react": "^19.0.0"`) for shared deps in any workspace package are forbidden — the catalog entry is the single source.
rule.rationale: A single hard-coded value in one package.json defeats the whole catalog mechanism.

rule.statement: CI MUST run `pnpm --filter '...[origin/main]'` to test only changed packages plus their dependents.
rule.rationale: Workspace-wide `pnpm test` on every commit is unnecessary; filtered runs cut wall-clock time 5–20x.

rule.statement: Pre-release versions (canary / rc / beta) MUST live in a named catalog (e.g., `catalogs.next.react`) referenced as `"catalog:next"`, never in the default catalog.
rule.rationale: Named catalogs let one or two packages opt into the canary without affecting the rest.

rule.statement: Repos that publish to npm MUST run a publish step that replaces `catalog:` and `workspace:*` with concrete versions before tarball build.
rule.rationale: Catalog and workspace protocols are pnpm-only; npm registry rejects them on publish.

rule.statement: If repo is single-package OR uses Bun-native lockfile OR is a Yarn 4 PnP shop with working constraints, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when catalogs add ceremony without payoff.
